textPlain: Even as someone who doesn't mind writing in C, I would absolutely flag that function as way, way, way, way too terse for no good reason.The code gets ridiculously easier to read if you write src[0] and src[1] instead of (*src) and (*(src+1)).  And, as a bonus, the precedence problem disappears.I really don't understand people why write C code like the original code.  It's just asking for a bug.

There's another bug, where you don't update psrc in those error return cases.  The parser will be stuck at the malformed % forever.  Or maybe that is desired; it's hard to tell.The precedence stuff here is pretty basic.  When in doubt, using parentheses to make order explicit is never wrong.  Or consult https://en.cppreference.com/w/c/language/operator_precedence... .
In general avoid frivolous use of pointer arithmetic. foo[k] and *(foo+k) will usually generate identical asm, and the former is just easier to read…
And for the love of God, please don't do 0[src].
